evidence: File begins with `# Scaffold New Project` (h1); no YAML frontmatter block found; pattern applies to all 26 command files.
description: All 26 command files lack YAML frontmatter; name and description fields required for NLPM registration are absent; each file incurs -55 base penalty (name -25, description -25, allowed-tools -5).
suggested_fix:
Add frontmatter to every command file: `---
name: <slug>
description: <one-line purpose>
allowed-tools: [<tools used>]
---`

description: Plugin name is 'atm' but example invocations in command bodies use '/atm-scaffold' (hyphen); Claude Code registered paths would use 'atm:scaffold' (colon) or the bare name; inconsistent documentation confuses users.
suggested_fix: Standardise all example invocations to the actual registered form (e.g. '/atm:scaffold' for scoped installs) and add a Usage section to README.md.

description: Python script written to /tmp/claude-filter.py and executed via subprocess in a git filter-repo callback; file is constructed by Claude and never persists between runs.
fp_reason: Claude Code execution context already has user-level filesystem access; writing to /tmp and executing a self-constructed script is not a privilege escalation vector in this threat model.
rule_gap: Rule should distinguish self-constructed ephemeral scripts from scripts whose content derives from external or user-controlled input.

description: APP_PATH set from $ARGUMENTS and passed to grep without sanitization; same pattern in angular-architecture-audit, angular-performance-audit, angular-style-audit, django-api-audit, django-model-audit (6 files total).
fp_reason: grep runs inside the user's own checked-out repository with no cross-user boundary; worst case is the user auditing a directory they chose themselves.
rule_gap: Rule should account for the absence of a privilege boundary when the tool runs as the user who invoked it.

evidence: 'args': ['-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp'] — no version specifier; npx -y will install the latest published version on every MCP server start.
description: MCP server declaration uses 'npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp' without a version pin; any malicious or breaking publish of the package silently affects all plugin users.
suggested_fix: Pin to a specific release: 'args': ['-y', '@upstash/context7-mcp@X.Y.Z'] and update when a new tested version is released.

evidence: File starts at line 1 with '# Scaffold New Project'; no YAML frontmatter delimiters found. Representative file; all 26 command files share this defect.
description: Missing name (-25) and description (-25) in frontmatter across all 26 command files; allowed-tools also absent (-5); combined base penalty -55 per file applies to every command.
suggested_fix: Add YAML frontmatter block at line 1 of each command file with name, description, and allowed-tools fields.

evidence: File body sets a persona ('You are now operating as a senior accessibility engineer...') then lists topics; no numbered steps or ordered workflow present. Same pattern in angular-expert.md and django-expert.md.
description: Expert persona commands (a11y-expert, angular-expert, django-expert) define no numbered process steps; the model has no deterministic execution order for multi-step tasks.
suggested_fix: Add a numbered step sequence even for persona-mode commands, e.g. '1. Confirm scope 2. Audit area X 3. Report findings'.

evidence: SPIKE_TOPIC=$ARGUMENTS used at the top of the bash block with no preceding empty check; if invoked bare the variable is empty and SPIKE_ID derivation silently produces a malformed slug.
description: $ARGUMENTS consumed without empty check; worst-case file shown (spike-investigation scores 15); same defect in pr-review, feature-investigation, implement-pr-feedback, problem-solver, release-architect (6 files total).
suggested_fix: Add at the top of each affected command: if [[ -z "$ARGUMENTS" ]]; then echo "Usage: /atm:<command> <required-arg>"; exit 1; fi

evidence: FEATURE_ID=$ARGUMENTS assigned with no guard; downstream AC-gate step silently operates on empty string if invoked without arguments.
description: $ARGUMENTS used without empty-input guard; -10 penalty combines with 8 vague quantifiers (-16) to produce score 19.
suggested_fix: Add empty guard before FEATURE_ID assignment; display usage hint referencing JIRA ticket ID format.

evidence: 'pbcopy' appears in the step instruction for copying the commit message; pbcopy is a macOS-only command not available on Linux or Windows.
description: pbcopy used without OS detection fallback; command fails silently on non-macOS platforms; the bash skill documents a cross-platform clipboard abstraction that is not applied here.
suggested_fix: Use $OSTYPE guard: pbcopy on darwin*, xclip -selection clipboard or xsel --clipboard --input on linux*; or reference the bash skill clipboard helper.

evidence: 'Always' appears 3 times as an unconditional directive; 'graceful', 'informative', 'standard' appear without measurable thresholds.
description: 6 vague quantifiers in bash skill (Always×3, graceful, informative, standard) without observable success criteria; reduces scoring precision for commands that load this skill.
suggested_fix: Replace 'Always check tool availability' with 'Before using <tool>, test with command -v <tool> >/dev/null 2>&1 and exit 1 with an install hint if not found'.

evidence: effective, specific, appropriate, focused, relevant, clear, good, proper each appear at least once without measurable criteria.
description: 8 vague quantifiers in the ai skill; highest vague penalty of the two skill files; reduces instruction precision for commands that reference this skill for prompt-writing guidance.
suggested_fix: Replace subjective adjectives with observable criteria, e.g. 'specific examples' → 'at least two concrete input/output examples per behaviour described'.
